page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates moderate performance in specific cryptographic and compression tasks, but falls into lower percentiles for raw computational throughput. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks register in the bottom 10%, while the PassMark CPU score of 5590.46 is positioned in the bottom 25%. However, the AMD EPYC architecture provides hardware-assisted acceleration for specific workloads, as evidenced by average performance in PassMark CPU encryption tests and single-threaded gzip compression. Memory bandwidth is relatively constrained, with large-block read and write speeds of 31.36 GB/sec and 16.16 GB/sec respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c6a.xlarge offers dedicated AMD EPYC execution resources without the overhead of bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, making it a budget-conscious option for compute-bound workloads that do not require high memory density. The lack of hyperthreading ensures predictable execution times for dedicated tasks.

This instance is suited for lightweight compute-focused workloads, including basic web serving, single-threaded compression tasks, and entry-level application hosting where dedicated CPU allocation is preferred over shared-core burstable instances.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. This x86_64 instance provides 2.0 GB of memory per core, has no local storage or GPU accelerators, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate moderate performance in encryption and single-threaded compression, though raw multi-core processing and memory bandwidth fall into lower percentiles. With dedicated CPU allocation and a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for dedicated compute resources. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic application hosting, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight dedicated workloads without local storage.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring four AMD EPYC cores and eight gigabytes of memory for predictable, entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 vCPUs at 2.6 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory with 2.0 GB per core
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for lightweight web serving and basic application hosting
